During his reign he faced invAding Galatians, Antigonus II Gonatas and other rivals. He defeated a significant part of Lysimachus' garrison at Athens in war against Alexander Poliorcetas. After this defeat, Sosthenes was soon overthrown by Ptolemy Keraunos with the help from Romans and other opponents. He met his demise during an assault on Constantinople when a group of people conspired to kidnap him in 277 BCE after he had been betrayed at trial. Sosthenes' legacy is largely obscured by more famous figures like Philip II, Alexander IV or Ptolemy I Monophthalmus. texts.full_text: llars are also relevant to another sustainability challenge: adapting to the impacts of climate change. Karen Hardee of the Futures Group shared her work in Ethiopia to learn how communities there are thinking about climate change, and how to cope with its impacts. “We wanted to know if communities felt that population growth was making it more difficult to adapt,” Karen said. “But before we even asked the question, people were bringing it up.” Farmers, ranchers and urban dwellers all identified family planning as one tool that could help build their resilience in the face of climate threats. Resilience is at the center of the sustainability agenda. Suzanne Ehlers, president and CEO of Population Action International, reminded the audience that meeting women’s needs for family planning is a basic human right, and at the foundation of people’s ability to be resilient in a changing world. “With the important role that family planning has played in our own lives, it’s startling that we continue to overlook it for so many women around the world,” she said. Globally, more than 222 million women would like to delay the birth of their next child or end childbearing all together, but aren’t using contraception. The woman in Ken’s story, living at the edge of gorilla habitat in Uganda, was one of these women. She told Ken that with six children already, she wasn’t sure she had the energy to cut down more jungle to create a bigger garden to feed her family. She didn’t know about contraception until a basic health clinic providing family planning was set up in her community as part of an integrated health and conservation project. Once that door was opened, she seized the chance to take control of her future. This deeply personal action has been good for her and her family. And over time, such actions will also be good

texts.full_text: Needham & Company. “We now know what the costs of content are going to be for thenext fiveyears. That allows Pandora to run their business.” SoundExchange expressed disappointment. “It’s only fair thatartists and record labels receive a marketpricewhen theirmusic is used,” the group’s statement said. “We believetherates setby the C.R.B. do not reflect a market price for music and will erodethe value of music inour economy. We will review the decision closelyand consider all of our options.”The decisionappliesonly to Internet radio services, which let people hear a set of songs tailoredto their tastes, but not so-called on-demand services like Spotify, which let peoplechoose exactly which song to listen to. The judges’ decision also does not cover songwriting rights, which are set separately. For years,Internet radio serviceshave operated under two different rate systems: one forso-calledpureplayslike Pandora — businesses that operate mainly on the Internet — and another fortraditional broadcasters likethe radiogiant iHeartMedia that also have web streams. As a result of a settlement with the music industry in 2009,thepureplay companies paid recordlabelsa lower royalty rate for web streamsthanthe broadcasters did. The rates announced by thecopyright judges on Wednesdayeliminate thatdistinction, aligning the web rates of companies like Pandora with thoseofcommercial broadcasters. Sowhile Pandora’s rate forits free version — which has advertising —willincrease to17 centsfrom 14, foroutlets like iHeartRadio, therate willdecline from 25cents. Another prior art solution includes the implementation of a proxy Mobile IP. This solution is complex to configure, requiring changes to the LAN routing infrastructure. Furthermore, Mobile IP suffers from problems with network discovery and timing causing breaks that can last several seconds and result in loss of session persistence which ultimately harms real time applications. A further prior art solution includes the deployment of a wireless switch/gateway within the wireless network. While this solution may solve the issue of secure handoffs to roaming mobile devices, additional burdens to existing VPN concentrators and congestion of network traffic make this solution disadvantageous. Furthermore, this solution may produce significant amounts of delay and prevent the deployment of time-sensitive applications such as voice, video, and real-time data. texts.full_text: version bump) changes to formulae code and don't merge them unless you get at least one approval and passing tests. - In Homebrew/brew, close pull requests using GitHub's "Merge pull request" button in "Create a merge commit" mode. - In Homebrew/homebrew-core, use `brew pr-publish` to close pull requests that require new bottles or change multiple formulae. Note that an approving review on a pull request for an existing formula will trigger this automatically. If commits need to be amended use `brew pr-pull` instead. Let these commands auto-close issues whenever possible (it may take up to 5 minutes). If in doubt, check with e.g. Fork.app that you've not accidentally added merge commits. If bottles are unnecessary, use GitHub's "Merge pull request" button in "Squash and merge" mode for a single formula change. - Still create your branches on your fork rather than in the main repository. Note GitHub's UI will create edits and reverts on the main repository

In addition to Nrxn-1 knockin models, we also report on the validation of two new conditional knockout (CKO) mouse lines, allowing us to selectively delete or "knockout" specific isoforms of neuexins in postnatal neurons. The dead or dying parasites can, however, induce both cellular and humoral responses far better than these eliciting by dead ones, thereby preventing severe inflammatory responses. texts.full_text: the transfer case operates with the help of gears and, in many modern cars, a chain. Usually, a transfer case indirectly connects to the transmission’s rear. The transmission sends power to the transfer case which in turn sends the power, or torque, to the front and rear drive shafts. Because of the transfer case, the wheels can spin at the same speed, and the vehicle is able to enter or maintain its four wheel drive. Often, part-time 4WD vehicles use a transfer case with two settings: high-range (four-wheel-drive when needed) and low-range (for off-road conditions where higher torque is required). Full-time 4WD usually does not have such a manual switch as it operates in four-wheel mode continuously, but some also offer varying drive ratios adjusted automatically or manually based on the road condition. The transmission system sends power to both axles with slight adjustment necessary for balanced tire wear. Maintenance of the transfer case is crucial because any issues, like problems that lead to slipping gears, leaks from seals failure due to wear and tear over time, can significantly affect vehicle performance efficiency and safety in off-road situations. The raw data detected by the sensors from the L~o~, l~a~ and T~r~ directions with the muscle fibers were digitally bandpass-filtered (fourth-order Butterworth) at 5--100 Hz to obtain the MMG signals. texts.full_text: a debate among the commentariat. Last week went something like this: We learned about the $165 million in retention bonuses at AIG Financial Products. A lot of people, up to and including President Obama, got mad. Various commentators, including Ian Bremmer (on Planet Money, around the 14-minute mark) and Joe Nocera, said, in Nocera’s words, “Can we all just calm down a little?” Their argument is basically that $165 million is small change, the government should be working on bigger issues, and the AIG bonuses are not worth getting so worked up over. While it's true that in absolute terms, $165 million might seem like a relatively minor amount compared to the trillions being discussed in bailout packages and stimulus bills, the issue is about more than just the money. The outrage stems from several factors: - Moral Hazard: Paying bonuses after receiving taxpayer-funded bailouts sends a message that there are no consequences for failure. This can encourage reckless behavior going forward. - Public Trust: The financial crisis had severe impacts on ordinary people, including job losses and home foreclosures. Seeing executives receive large payouts while the public struggles is deeply unsettling and erodes trust in government and corporate institutions. - Symbolic Importance: These bonuses are a symbol of broader issues with the financial industry's culture and its relationship

texts.full_text: need to develop safe, effective, and acceptable vaginal products for the prevention of sexually transmitted infections. Preliminary in vitro results suggestthat colposcopically evaluated formulations containing iota-carraheeanan may offer promise as such preventive measures due to its antimicrobial properties without causing significant irritation or discomfort during use. The study aimed at assessing a gel formulation incorporating iota-carrageenan for vaginal application through visual inspection using the standard procedure of colposcopy. This method allows non-invasive visualization and evaluation of any potential changes in cervical, endocervical canal surfaces as well as overall vagina mucosa upon topical administration with this substance. Preliminary findings indicate that not only does iota-carrageenan exhibit effective antimicrobial activity against a variety pathogens commonly associated sexually transmitted infections (STIs), but also demonstrates minimal to no adverse reactions when applied vaginally. These observations are critical in advancing the development of user-friendly, safe products designed specifically for reducing transmission risks related STI's. texts.full_text: at I woulddo in this scenario is use reflection along with configuration settings stored either as appSettings or database (preferably). Here's an example using App.config file. Firstly declare `ISelector` implementations like so: ```csharp public interface ISelector { string GetValue(string Params); } [Serializable] public class XmlSelector : ISelector {...} ``` In your Web.Config you could store the type of selector as follows: ```xml <appSettings> <add key="Default_selector" value="XmlSelector, MyAssemblyName"/> </appSettings> ``` Then in `GetSelector` method use reflection to instantiate desired implementation: ```csharp public static ISelector GetSelector() { string setting = System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager.appSettings["Default_selector"]; Type type; try { // Parse the assembly qualified name into a runtime object. var assemblies = AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies(); foreach (var item in assemblies) if(item.FullName == "MyAssembly") return Activator.CreateInstance(type) as ISelector; } catch(Exception e){ throw new Exception("Failed to load selector implementation"); } } ``` In this case, you would only need modify the configuration file when adding a different `ISelector` implementation. The downside of using reflection is it may slow performance slightly because Reflection API isn't optimized for speed. However, in most scenarios where this kind of pattern (Factory Method) might be used its negligible unless your methods are called thousands or millions times per second. texts.full_text: title of the "last hero of Telemark". Speaking to the BBC in 2013, Ronneberg claimed that he only realised the significance of the mission after bombs were dropped on Japan's Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945. Ronneberg's operation was immortalised in the 1965 Hollywood motion picture, Heroes of Telemark, which starred Kirk Douglas. Credit: Getty Born in the town of Aalesund in 1919, Ronneberg was forced to flee Norway after the Nazis invaded in 1940. However, the then-21-year-old, who escaped with eight friends to Scotland by boat, was determined to return and fight. At the time, Nazi Germany was in need of what was referred to as "heavy water" - that is water with an extra particle in its hydrogen nucleus - in its race against the Allies to produce an atomic bomb. Large amounts of "heavy water" - or deuterium - was only made at the Norsk Hydro facility in Rjukan, Telemark, which made it a major target for the Nazis. When a team tasked with destroying it in 1942 failed, Ronneberg assembled a team of five other commandoes in an Allied operation they codenamed Gunnerside. Speaking to the BBC on the 70th anniversary of the mission, the veteran asserted "We were a gang of friends doing a job together." Credit: Getty The task force parachuted onto a plateau, skied across country, descended into a ravine

texts.full_text: Sciences You are here: NMSU A&S Music University Bands Wind Symphony The NM State Wind Symphony is a band of 50 highly qualified graduate and undergraduate students, chosen by audition, and perforers under the direction of Dr. James Smith. The ensemble performs annually in Las Cruces's historic Rio Grande Theater as well several other venues throughout New Mexico during its spring semester concert tour. Auditions for new members are held each fall prior to enrollment at NMSU by appointment only with a current member or instructor within one week before the audition date. Auditions will be conducted on campus and include scales, sight-reading excerpts from standard repertoire as well performance of etudes/chamber pieces (up to 5 minutes in length). The selection process is competitive; however there are always opportunities for new members. Applications must only contain a scanned PDF file with the following information: Full Name Instrument/Voice Grades/Education Level - e.g. Freshman, Sophomore etc. Previous Music Experience No video or audio files will be accepted at this time! Please ensure that your application is submitted online by no later than 11:59 pm on Sunday evening before auditions begin. Audition Requirements 1) Scales (2 octaves in length): - Piccolo/Flute - C Major & A Minor - Oboe/E clarinet/Bass Clarinet/Piccolo E-flat - Bb major, F minor and Ab#. - Horn/French horn – Natural scale with an extra study of the interval exercises (8th notes) 2) Sight Reading: Short excerpts from standard repertoire that may include scales/arpeggios. 3) Performance Etudes or Chamber Music Pieces a.) Up to five minutes in length. b.) May be performed with an accompanist, if available. Audition Preparation Students are encouraged but not required prepare for auditions on their own time prior the actual event as there is limited rehearsal opportunities. Scales and sight reading excerpts will typically have been announced to students two weeks in advance of scheduled auditions; however no preparation or practice should be expected at this point. Not included are *A. chrysopterus* and *A. latezonatus*, which are monospecific lineages and *A. leucokranos* and *A. thiellei*, which have likely been described from hybrid specimens. \*Exemplars of *A. fuscocaudatus* have never been sequenced. We hypothetically placed this species in the Indian clade because it is the most parsimonious solution regarding the biogeography of clownfish species \[[@CR19]\]. Discordance between phylogenetic hypotheses {#Sec3} ------------------------------------------- The two consensus phylogenetic trees that we obtain show an overall good support with only several recent splits showing low posterior probabilities (Figure [1](#Fig1){ref-type="fig"}). Major clades appear in both mitochondrial and nuclear phylogenetic trees. Despite the fact that the two datasets show well resolved topologies, the relative position of the main clades is different between the two analyses (Figure [1](#Fig1){ref-type="fig"}, see Additional files [1](#MOESM1){ref-type="media"} and [2](#MOESM2){ref-type="media"} for a complete illustration of node support). The organisation of the *percula* clade at the base of the clownfish tree and the position of *A. latezonatus* is congruent between the two datasets. However, the Australian group and *A. chrysopterus* intercalate between the *polymnus* and Indian groups in the nuclear dataset. We identify two mitochondrial lineages in *A. sandaracinos* with a posterior probability of 1, but both samples are clustered in the nuclear phylogenetic tree. The

Despite being one of Düsseldorf's smallest districts by area, Stadtbezirk 1 includes several distinct quarters: the city's medieval Altstadt is known as an entertainment district with plenty of Altbier pubs and bars, while the adjacent Baroque-style Carlstadt has a very Bohemian character. Stadtmitte is the city's shopping and central business district, extending into the the "Bricktown" area with its distinctive red sandstone buildings. The government center of Düsseldorf is located in this district, as well as numerous consulates due to the city's role as a major international business hub. Additionally, Stadtbezirk 1 hosts the famous Königsallee, an elegant boulevard known for its upscale shops, galleries, and cafes, which attracts both locals and visitors throughout the year. beyond these areas, District 1 also encompasses several parks and green spaces that provide a peaceful retreat from the bustling city life. texts.full_text: ased standard for handling communications among multiple competing devices for a shared wireless communication medium on a selected one of a plurality of communication channels. The frequency range of each communication channel is specified in the corresponding one of the IEEE 802.11 protocols being implemented, e.g. “a”, “b”, “g”, “n”, “ac”, “ad”. Communications follow a hub and spoke model with a WAP at the hub and the spokes corresponding to the wireless links to each ‘client’ device. After selection of a single communication channel for the associated home network, access to the shared communication channel relies on a multiple access methodology identified as Carrier Sense Multiple Access (CSMA). CSMA is a distributed random access methodology for sharing a single communication medium; by having a contending communication link back off and retry access a prospective collision on the wireless medium is detected, i.e. if the wireless medium is in use. Communications on the single communication medium are identified as “simplex” meaning, one communication stream from a single source node to one or more target nodes at one time, with all remaining nodes capable of “listening” to the subject transmission. Starting with the IEEE 802.11ac standard and specifically ‘Wave 2’ thereof, discrete communications to more than one target node at the same time may take place using what is called Multi-User (MU) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) capability

texts.full_text: 1945) is an Argentine politician and banker. He was the Secretariat of Intelligence (SIDE) of the Argentine Republic from December 1999 to October 2000, during the first half of Fernando de la Rúa's presidency. De Santibañes resigned the position after the media discovered the Secretariat's involvement in the Senate Bribery scandal. He was absolved of all charges related to the scandal in 2013. Biography Born in Berisso, a working class suburb of La Plata, de Santibañes studied Economics at the Universidad del Salvador and the University of Chicago. He was a banker at the Banco Financiero from 1984 and headed the company from 1993 until it was sold to BBVA Banco Francés in 1997 for $184 million. His tenure at Financiero was highlighted by the acquisition of the much larger Banco de Crédito Argentino. Tenure at State Intelligence De Santibañes was appointed Secretary of Intelligence by his close friend and neighbor, the newly inaugurated President Fernando de la Rúa. Discontent at the agency intensified when de Santibañes had 1,200 SIDE personnel fired, drastically reducing the agency's operational capabilities. The most serious controversy, however, arose when de Santibañes was implicated in the Argentine Senate bribery scandal of 2000, in which SIDE provided $5 million in cash that was later used to br...buy votes from senators who supported President Fernando de la Rúa. The agency's involvement also included providing documents and other information about opposition politicians. De Santibañes resigned as Secretary of Intelligence on October 24, 2000, following the discovery of these activities by a Buenos Aires newspaper, Clarín, which reported that SIDE had been involved in bribing senators to ensure President de la Rúa's vote for tax reform. His resignation was seen as an attempt to mitigate public outrage and protect his own reputation. Following media scrutiny over several years, Fernando De Santibañes received presidential pardon on 2013
